rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF TOMMY HUANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/29/21. (No earlier availability.) Examiner Notes (issues necessitating continuance): Still no notice has been given to the minor. [Probate Code Section 1460(b)(2) and CRC 7.51(d).] The caption of the pleading filed on 3/23/21 was "Amended Petition for Approval of Second Account Current and Report of Guardian of Estate for Period April 1, 2018 Through March 31, 2020; Payment of Attorney Fees; and Payment of Guardian of Estate Fees." This was not the verbatim caption shown on the Notice of Hearing. Other issues posted on 4/5/21 have not been addressed: It appears that the ending balance of assets on hand should be corrected as a result of the Probate Referee's appraisal. The guardian's time entries reflect that much of her time was spent on real property related issues. Is this estate responsible for the full amount of her fees? (Exhibit B to the amended petition does not actually support disbursements attributable to this ward; it merely shows an amount paid by one of the guardians of the person. Paragraph 1 of the amended petition is also incorrect as to who the guardian of the person is.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B. Courtesy copies of any responsive papers must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 6/18/21.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF VIRGINIA DAVES HOOK
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although attorney costs were not set forth in sufficient detail (date, amount, payee, description) for the Court to determine if they were justified. Since the conservator is the sole beneficiary of the trust that received the balance of the conservatorship estate, the Court will not require further support for such costs. The four reimbursements (by way of payments directly made) by the Bypass Trust for the amounts inadvertently paid out of the conservatorship estate also do not appear to equal the total amount inadvertently paid, as shown in paragraph 12. (It was also noted that the Probate Code Section 13100 affidavits filed in this case did not have a certified copy of a death certificate attached to them; however, if the financial institutions honored the affidavits, the Court does not need to have full affidavits filed.) NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution," or "Order Terminating Conservatorship [or Guardianship or Trust]." [LRSF 14.36 I.6.b.] Please make a note of it.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES FREDERICK DAUGHERTY

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROSE PAK
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/29/21. (No earlier availability.) Examiner Notes (issues necessitating continuance): The second amended petition filed on 4/20/21 did not, in fact, include an accounting. [CRC 7.3(3).] The accounting period exceeding the period this Court allows still has not been changed, as noted in the issues posted in the tentative ruling for 2/2/21. [LRSF 14.35 A.] The I&A amount purported to be the "Partial No. 1 as corrected" shown in paragraph 18 still has not been corrected and is not supported by the I&A filed on 11/6/17 and the (partially correcting) one filed on 6/9/20. Only the original financial statements verifying the ending balances in a decedent's estate need to be filed. [LRSF 14.35 D.] Whenever originals (computer generated or traditional) are E-filed, the attorney or the fiduciary who retains the original must file a declaration certifying that the original document(s) will be retained in the same way as documents are retained under Local Rule 2.11 M.4., which provides that the original(s) must be retained "for at least the period after the termination, including all appeals, of the case required for conventionally filed documents." Courtesy copies of either the (complete) third amended account or a third amended petition on waiver of account if the two heirs-at-law will waive an accounting, must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 6/18/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: JOHN BERTRAND & VIRGINIA DAVES HOOK REVOCABLE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although attorney costs were not set forth in sufficient detail (date, amount, payee, description) for the Court to determine if they were justified. Since the successor trustee is the sole beneficiary, the Court will not require further support for such costs. It is noted, however, that fees and costs associated with filing all bank statements may not be reasonable, when only the statements verifying the ending balances shown in the accounting were required. It is also unclear why the fans were not distributed to the specific devisee more than seven months after the death of the surviving settlor.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF HATTIE BARNES
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/29/21. (No earlier availabilty.) Incomplete response to issues posted in the preliminary tentative ruling posted on 4/9/21: The amounts shown on the JC form Summary of Account for Schedule A and Schedule C are not as shown on the supporting schedules. The date of any order authorizing fees must be set forth. No informational schedule was attached regarding property in another state. Will the conservator not seek reimbursement for funds she contributed (no liabilities)? The accounting period ends 6/30/20, but the assets on hand on Schedule E appear to be for later dates, which could be part of the reason why the accounting did not balance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GUILLERMO COBAR
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (588 - 12th Ave, San Francisco, Ca)
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The date of death is missing at item 3a of the report. Proof of publication filed on 5/5/2021 is purportedly a "Notice of Intention to Sell Real Property" but it is for this hearing date to confirm the sale. No notice was given to William L. Walker, who represents Celtic Research, Ltd., and filed a RSN on 8/29/19. POS filed on 5/6/21 shows that notice was mailed 5/6/21, which was less than 15 days before this hearing, and notice was not given to the purchasers. No Reappraisal for Sale has been filed yet. The amount bid is not at least 90% of the (date of death) appraised value. The overbid amount is miscalculated. Due to the COVID-19 emergency, counsel and parties must appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CLAUDE L HEATER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/15/2021. Examiner Notes: 1) When a personal representative, who is an attorney, requests fees for services as the attorney in addition to the personal representative's compensation, Court approval must be requested within ninety (90) days after Letters are issued to the attorney as the personal representative. The petition for approval of such additional statutory fees must be set on the regular hearing calendar and must set forth specifically why it would be to the advantage, benefit, and best interests of the decedent's estate. Probate Code 10804. [LRSF 14.56]. 2) Proof of satisfaction of the creditor claim must be filed separately and signed in their capacity as trustee. 3) DE-174 must be filed separately. 4) Paragraph 16 must state the character of the property of Decedent. 5) The proposed order must begin with Order for Final Distribution. [LRSF 14.36 I. 6) b.] 6) Please amend the calculation of fees to conform to CRC 7.705 or file legal authority in support of rounding up the total fee base when calculating statutory compensation. 7) The Attorney General's Office must be given notice of the petition as there is a charitable devise to Claude Heater Foundation. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 6/1/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CALVIN GA MING YU
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: continued to 7/14/21 (first available date for the examiner). no later than noon on July 7, a supplement to the petition must be filed (signed and verified by the petitioner) that states whether or not petitioner has been contacted by an entity that received notice under PC 9202 just prior to the petition being filed (petition filed well before time to respond or file a claim had run). in addition, the supplement must address why a determination of whether or not a lawsuit will be filed re wrongful death has not been made yet, since the decedent died 6/12/20, almost a year ago. finally, the supplement must address why the estate cannot be closed, but the personal representatives not discharged with any proceeds passing through an omnibus clause.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MURIEL FOX TRUST DATED JULY 23, 1996
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Modifying Terms Of Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/22/2021. 1) The Supplemental Declaration of Anna Michnicka was an unfiled copy and has been recycled. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 2) Please give 30 days' notice to the charitable organizations listed on page 4 of the trust article 4 section B. (2): St. Ignatius Prepatory School, St. Anthony Foundation, and the Salvation Army. (Probate Code 1202). 3) Please give 30 days' notice to the California attorney general. (Probate Code 1202).
